news.headline: Aberdeen Group plc (abrdn) discloses 2.85M-share, 4.52% holding in uniQure
news.summary: Aberdeen Group plc filed a Schedule 13G disclosing a passive stake of roughly 2.85 million uniQure ordinary shares, equal to about 4.52% of shares outstanding, down from the over-8% position it reported in March 2026. The reduction reflects both dilution from June's offering and some active trimming after the stock's post-FDA-reversal rally.

news.headline: JPMorgan Chase & Co. discloses 7.0% passive stake in uniQure
news.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. filed a Schedule 13G disclosing beneficial ownership of approximately 4,866,211 uniQure ordinary shares, roughly 7.0% of shares outstanding, held as a passive institutional investment. The filing is part of a broader run of large institutional-ownership disclosures following the stock's post-FDA-reversal rally.

news.headline: uniQure CEO and other executives/directors sell shares after option exercises under 10b5-1 plans
news.summary: CEO Matthew Kapusta exercised options at $19.39 and sold the resulting 21,082 ordinary shares at roughly $44-$46 under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, leaving him with 519,227 shares. Director Jack Kaye similarly exercised and sold about 21,167 shares around $46, and CMO Walid Abi-Saab sold roughly 30,000 shares on June 30 under comparable 10b5-1 plans.

news.headline: uniQure announces closing of upsized public offering and full exercise by underwriters of option to purchase additional shares
news.summary: uniQure closed its underwritten offering of 5,686,813 ordinary shares at $45.50 per share, upsized from an initially announced $150 million raise and including the underwriters' full option exercise, for gross proceeds of approximately $259 million. Proceeds are earmarked to fund AMT-130 commercialization readiness, the required confirmatory study, and the broader pipeline.

news.headline: uniQure announces preliminary data on the first cohort in the Phase I/IIa clinical trial of AMT-260 in refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy
news.summary: Six-month follow-up data on the first, low-dose AMT-260 cohort (six patients) showed the therapy was generally well tolerated with no serious adverse events, and three of six patients had meaningful reductions (79%-100%) in disabling seizures. Enrollment in the higher-dose cohort is ongoing, with updated data from both cohorts expected in H1 2027.

news.headline: uniQure announces plan for BLA submission for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease; stock surges 78% as analysts race to raise targets
news.summary: The FDA informed uniQure during a Type B meeting that the ~3-year Phase I/II data (75% slowing of cUHDRS progression vs. external control, p=0.003) can serve as the primary basis for a BLA seeking accelerated approval, reversing its earlier stance requiring a new randomized/sham-controlled trial; uniQure plans to submit the BLA in Q3 2026. Shares surged about 78% on the news, and in the following days Barclays, Cantor Fitzgerald, RBC, Leerink, Goldman Sachs, H.C. Wainwright and Mizuho all sharply raised price targets (up to $80).

catalysts.label: AMT-130 BLA submission (US)
catalysts.detail: uniQure plans to submit a Biologics License Application to the FDA seeking accelerated approval of AMT-130 for Huntington's disease, based on ~3-year Phase I/II data accepted by the FDA as the primary efficacy basis.

catalysts.label: UK MHRA MAA filing for AMT-130
catalysts.detail: Following a successful pre-submission meeting, uniQure plans to file a Marketing Authorization Application with the UK MHRA for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: uniQure is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and provide a pipeline/regulatory update in this window, per data-provider estimates; as of July 21 the company has not yet issued a formal date-announcement press release.

catalysts.label: FDA alignment on confirmatory study design
catalysts.detail: Before the BLA submission, uniQure still needs to finalize the design of the required post-approval confirmatory study with the FDA, including whether it uses a concurrent standard-of-care control arm instead of a sham-surgery placebo (an approach patient advocates and some ethicists have pushed for, while some observers argue a standard-of-care-only control raises its own ethical and interpretability concerns); the FDA has said it will work expeditiously with the company on this.

catalysts.label: 4-year AMT-130 data analysis
catalysts.detail: uniQure expects to complete a 4-year analysis of the Phase I/II AMT-130 data, following FDA feedback on the statistical analysis plan discussed at the Type B meeting.

catalysts.label: AMT-260 higher-dose cohort enrollment completion
catalysts.detail: Enrollment in the second, higher-dose (3x10^12 gc/mL) AMT-260 cohort of six refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy patients is expected to complete around mid-2026.

catalysts.label: AMT-260 updated data (both cohorts)
catalysts.detail: uniQure expects to present updated efficacy/safety results from both the low- and higher-dose AMT-260 cohorts in refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy.

catalysts.label: Potential FDA accelerated approval decision for AMT-130
catalysts.detail: If the BLA is submitted in Q3 2026 and granted priority review, a potential FDA approval decision could arrive around mid-2027; timing not yet company-confirmed.

catalysts.label: Additional international regulatory updates
catalysts.detail: uniQure is pursuing further regulatory pathways for AMT-130 in additional international markets beyond the US and UK, with updates expected in the second half of 2026.

pulse: uniQure shares surged roughly 78% in mid-June 2026 after the FDA reversed course and agreed that ~3-year Phase I/II data on AMT-130 can support an accelerated-approval BLA in Huntington's disease, triggering a wave of analyst upgrades (targets up to $80) and enabling a $259M capital raise to fund the filing. The stock has since consolidated in the high-$30s to mid-$40s (around $40 on July 21), with recent activity dominated by routine insider 10b5-1 sales and a run of large institutional 13G disclosures -- JPMorgan reporting a new 7.0% passive stake on July 16 and Aberdeen/abrdn trimming its position to 4.52% on July 20 after holding over 8% earlier in the year -- signaling heavy but shifting institutional interest post-rally. Markets are now awaiting the confirmed Q3 2026 AMT-130 BLA and UK MHRA filings, finalization of the FDA-mandated post-approval confirmatory study design (still contentious given ethical debate over sham-surgery vs. standard-of-care controls), and an as-yet-unconfirmed Q2 2026 earnings update expected in the July 28-August 3 window; sentiment remains bullish (Strong Buy consensus, average target in the high-$50s to $70s) but hinges on execution of the upcoming regulatory filings.
